description: Implement the LRS project per IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md. Each task (T1-T30) should be one PR-sized chunk. Follow the dependency graph exactly.
title: Implement the LRS project per IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md. Each task (T1-T30) should be one PR-sized chunk. Follow the dependency graph exactly.

description: Create the project skeleton and shared type definitions. DELIVERABLES: 1. pyproject.toml with deps: torch, transformers, reservoirpy, scipy, numpy, wandb, peft, datasets, ruff, mypy, pytest 2. src/ package layout: src/__init__.py, src/reservoir/, src/models/, src/training/, src/eval/, src/eval/benchmarks/, src/data/, configs/, tests/, scripts/ 3. src/types.py with shared protocols/dataclasses: ReservoirConfig dataclass, Reservoir protocol (step/reset), ModelWrapper protocol (forward/generate/get_hidden), BenchmarkExample dataclass (input/target/metadata), EvalResult dataclass (task/metric/value/config), Generator protocol, DataPipeline protocol (iterate/split) 4. Pre-commit config for ruff + mypy 5. Basic CI skeleton (GitHub Actions or Makefile with lint+test targets) ACCEPTANCE: pip install -e . works, pytest runs (even with 0 tests), linter passes, all protocols importable from src.types. REPO STRUCTURE: See IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md §7 for target layout. NO DEPENDENCIES - this is the first task.
title: T1: Project scaffolding & shared interfaces

description: Evaluate LLaMA-3.2-1B on full benchmark suite. Architecture control for the DeltaNet synergy hypothesis — LLaMA uses pure softmax attention (no DeltaNet). DELIVERABLES: 1. Load meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B via T3 model loader 2. Run through eval harness on ALL benchmarks (T5 synthetic suite) 3. Output: results JSON in results/baselines/llama32_1b.json 4. Script: scripts/run_baseline_llama.py or configs/baselines/llama32.yaml DEPENDS ON: T3 (model loading), T4 (eval harness), T5 (benchmarks) COMPUTE: GPU (~3h) ACCEPTANCE: Full results JSON.
title: T10: LLaMA-3.2-1B pure-attention baseline

description: Implement compressive memory baseline (Infini-attention or Titans/ATLAS style) on Qwen3.5-0.8B. This is Control 3 — the strongest non-reservoir memory baseline. DELIVERABLES: 1. Implement Infini-attention or Titans-style test-time memory module that integrates with Qwen3.5-0.8B    - Compressive memory that summarizes past KV cache into fixed-size memory    - Can reference Infini-attention paper (Munkhdalai et al. 2024) or Titans (Khodadadi et al. 2025) 2. Train adapter + memory module using LoRA training pipeline (T13) on same fine-tuning data as Track A 3. Evaluate on full benchmark suite, especially memory benchmarks 4. Output: results JSON in results/baselines/infini_attention.json 5. Implementation at src/models/infini_attention.py or src/models/titans.py DEPENDS ON: T3 (model loading), T4 (eval harness), T5 (benchmarks) COMPUTE: GPU (~15h for implementation + training + eval) ACCEPTANCE: Trained model, eval results on memory benchmarks, comparison vs T7.
title: T11: Infini-attention / Titans baseline

description: Implement the reservoir-LLM interface modules at src/reservoir/interface.py. These are the core modules that connect the ESN reservoir to the transformer. DELIVERABLES: 1. ReadProjection: Linear projection from reservoir state dim -> LLM hidden dim. Maps reservoir states into the LLM's representation space. 2. WriteHead: Linear projection from LLM hidden dim -> reservoir input dim. Extracts write signals from LLM hidden states to feed back into the reservoir. 3. CrossAttentionSidecar:    - Reservoir states serve as Key/Value, LLM hidden states serve as Query    - Insertable at configurable transformer layers (list of layer indices)    - Adds cross-attention output to LLM hidden state via gated residual 4. FiLM-style residual modulation as alternative injection method: scale/shift LLM hidden state based on reservoir state 5. All modules designed for frozen LLM + LoRA: gradient flows through interface params but NOT through reservoir weights 6. Tests in tests/test_interface.py: modules instantiate, forward pass produces correct shapes, gradient flows through interface but stops at reservoir boundary DEPENDS ON: T2 (ESN reservoir, for reservoir state shapes), T3 (model loading, for LLM hidden dims) FILE: src/reservoir/interface.py COMPUTE: CPU only
title: T12: Read/Write interface & cross-attention sidecar module

description: Implement the training pipeline for frozen LLM + LoRA + reservoir interface at src/training/lora_trainer.py. DELIVERABLES: 1. LoRATrainer class with:    - Frozen base LLM + LoRA adapters (via peft library)    - Trainable reservoir interface params (ReadProjection, WriteHead, CrossAttentionSidecar from T12)    - AdamW optimizer for LoRA + interface params only    - Gradient checkpointing for VRAM efficiency    - Mixed precision training (BF16/FP16)    - Wandb logging (loss, lr, grad norms, reservoir stats) 2. Configurable: LoRA rank, target modules (q_proj, v_proj, etc.), lr schedule (cosine with warmup), dataset mixing ratios 3. Checkpoint save/load (model + optimizer + scheduler state) 4. Training data loading from HuggingFace datasets (FineWeb subset + synthetic from T5) 5. Tests in tests/test_trainer.py: pipeline runs a 10-step smoke test without OOM (can mock GPU), loss decreases, checkpoints save/load DEPENDS ON: T3 (model loading for ModelWrapper) FILE: src/training/lora_trainer.py COMPUTE: CPU only (builds pipeline, no actual training). GPU smoke test optional.
title: T13: LoRA + adapter training pipeline

description: Implement the Echo State Network reservoir at src/reservoir/esn.py. DELIVERABLES: 1. ESN class with configurable: size (500-50K nodes), spectral_radius, leak_rate, input_scaling, sparsity 2. Sparse topology generators: Erdos-Renyi and small-world (Watts-Strogatz) 3. State evolution: step(x_t, w_t) -> r_t using leaky-integrator equation r_t = (1-alpha)*r_{t-1} + alpha*tanh(W_res*r_{t-1} + W_in*[x_t; w_t]) 4. Sparse matrix storage (scipy.sparse or torch.sparse) 5. Batched forward for GPU tensors 6. reset() to zero state 7. Unit tests in tests/test_esn.py: echo state property (spectral radius < 1 => state fading), performance (<1ms per step at 50K on CPU), state norm bounded over 10K steps DEPENDS ON: T1 (needs src/ package structure and Reservoir protocol from src/types.py) FILE: src/reservoir/esn.py ACCEPTANCE: All unit tests pass, implements Reservoir protocol from src/types.py.
title: T2: ESN reservoir core library

description: Implement unified model loading at src/models/loader.py. DELIVERABLES: 1. load_model(name, dtype, device) function supporting: Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B-Base, meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B, state-spaces/mamba2-1.3b 2. Automatic dtype selection (FP16/BF16 based on GPU capability) 3. Tokenizer wrapper with consistent encode/decode interface across model families 4. Layer-name inspection utility: list_layers(model) returning layer names, types, shapes 5. Specific utility to identify DeltaNet blocks in Qwen3.5 by name 6. ModelWrapper protocol implementation (forward/generate/get_hidden from src/types.py) 7. Unit tests in tests/test_loader.py: model loads (can mock for CI), forward pass produces logits of correct shape, DeltaNet layers identified DEPENDS ON: T1 (needs src/ package structure and ModelWrapper protocol) FILE: src/models/loader.py NOTE: Models will be downloaded on first use. For CI, use mocks. Smoke test on GPU optional.
title: T3: Model loading & tokenizer utilities

description: Implement the evaluation harness at src/eval/harness.py. DELIVERABLES: 1. EvalHarness class that runs any benchmark suite against any ModelWrapper 2. Support for: few-shot prompting (configurable n_shots), greedy and sampling decode, per-task metrics (exact_match, perplexity, accuracy) 3. Batch evaluation for throughput (configurable batch_size) 4. Output: standardized JSON with EvalResult objects (task/metric/value/config) 5. CLI entry point or script to run evaluations from config 6. Unit tests in tests/test_harness.py: harness runs a dummy benchmark against a dummy model, produces valid JSON DEPENDS ON: T1 (needs EvalResult, BenchmarkExample, ModelWrapper, Generator protocols from src/types.py) FILE: src/eval/harness.py ACCEPTANCE: Harness runs with mock model/benchmark, produces valid JSON results file.
title: T4: Evaluation harness

description: Implement benchmark generators at src/eval/benchmarks/. DELIVERABLES: 1. src/eval/benchmarks/memory.py — Memory benchmarks:    - passkey_retrieval(depth, n) — hide a passkey in text, query it    - variable_tracking(n_vars, n_distractors) — track variable assignments through noise    - associative_recall(n_pairs, delay) — store key-value pairs, query after delay 2. src/eval/benchmarks/computation.py — Computation benchmarks:    - multi_digit_arithmetic(n_digits, op) — addition/multiplication with carry    - modular_arithmetic(modulus, n_ops) — chained mod operations    - dyck_language(depth, n_types) — Dyck language recognition    - program_trace(n_steps) — trace variable state through synthetic program execution 3. src/eval/benchmarks/emergent.py — Emergent capability benchmarks:    - compositional_generalization(held_out_ops) — test unseen operator combos    - length_extrapolation(train_len, test_len) — train short, test long    - algorithmic_transfer() — transfer learned algorithms to new domains 4. All generators produce (input, target, metadata) tuples matching BenchmarkExample from src/types.py 5. Configurable difficulty via parameters 6. Tests in tests/test_benchmarks.py: each generator produces 1K+ valid examples, difficulty scales DEPENDS ON: T1 (needs BenchmarkExample dataclass and Generator protocol) FILES: src/eval/benchmarks/memory.py, computation.py, emergent.py
title: T5: Synthetic benchmark suite

description: Implement chaotic systems trajectory generation at src/data/chaos.py. DELIVERABLES: 1. Lorenz-63 integrator: dx/dt = sigma*(y-x), dy/dt = x*(rho-z)-y, dz/dt = x*y - beta*z    Default params: sigma=10, rho=28, beta=8/3. Known Lyapunov exponent lambda ~= 0.906. 2. Mackey-Glass delay-differential: dx/dt = beta*x(t-tau)/(1+x(t-tau)^n) - gamma*x(t)    Variable tau for difficulty control. 3. Kuramoto-Sivashinsky PDE: u_t = -u*u_x - u_xx - u_xxxx    Pseudospectral method, configurable domain size. 4. Common interface: generate(system, n_steps, dt, params) -> trajectory array 5. Train/val/test split utilities (non-overlapping temporal splits) 6. Normalization (per-channel z-score) 7. Lyapunov time computation for evaluation horizon calibration 8. Tests in tests/test_chaos.py: Lorenz Lyapunov exponent matches ~0.906 (within 5%), splits non-overlapping, data loads in <1s DEPENDS ON: T1 (needs DataPipeline protocol from src/types.py) FILE: src/data/chaos.py
title: T6: Chaotic systems data pipeline

description: Evaluate unmodified Qwen3.5-0.8B-Base on full benchmark suite. This is the PRIMARY reference point for all comparisons. DELIVERABLES: 1. Run Qwen3.5-0.8B-Base through the eval harness (T4) on ALL benchmarks:    - Synthetic benchmarks from T5 (memory, computation, emergent)    - Chaotic system prediction from T6    - Standard benchmarks if available (GSM8K, ARC) 2. Record: perplexity on held-out text, accuracy on all synthetic tasks, throughput (tokens/sec), VRAM usage 3. Output: complete results JSON in results/baselines/qwen35_vanilla.json 4. Script: scripts/run_baseline_qwen.py or configs/baselines/qwen35_vanilla.yaml DEPENDS ON: T3 (model loading), T4 (eval harness), T5 (benchmarks), T6 (chaos data) COMPUTE: GPU (~3h) ACCEPTANCE: Complete results JSON for all benchmark categories.
title: T7: Qwen3.5-0.8B-Base vanilla baselines

description: Apply YaRN-style RoPE scaling to Qwen3.5-0.8B and evaluate on long-context benchmarks. This is Control 1 — tests if simple positional scaling solves the memory gap. DELIVERABLES: 1. Implement YaRN-style RoPE scaling for Qwen3.5-0.8B (modify rope_scaling config in transformers) 2. Evaluate on long-context benchmarks: passkey retrieval at 32K/64K/128K+ positions, variable tracking at long range 3. Compare vs T7 vanilla on same benchmarks 4. Output: results JSON in results/baselines/qwen35_yarn.json 5. Script: scripts/run_baseline_yarn.py or configs/baselines/qwen35_yarn.yaml DEPENDS ON: T3 (model loading), T4 (eval harness), T5 (benchmarks) COMPUTE: GPU (~3h) ACCEPTANCE: Long-context eval results at 32K, 64K, 128K+ positions.
title: T8: Qwen3.5-0.8B + RoPE/YaRN context extension baseline

description: Evaluate Mamba-2 1.3B on full benchmark suite. This is Control 2 — tests whether SSMs natively solve the memory gap without reservoir augmentation. DELIVERABLES: 1. Load Mamba-2 1.3B (state-spaces/mamba2-1.3b) via T3 model loader 2. Run through eval harness on ALL benchmarks (T5 synthetic + T6 chaotic) 3. Direct comparison table vs T7 (Qwen3.5 vanilla) 4. Output: results JSON in results/baselines/mamba2_1.3b.json 5. Script: scripts/run_baseline_mamba.py or configs/baselines/mamba2.yaml DEPENDS ON: T3 (model loading), T4 (eval harness), T5 (benchmarks) COMPUTE: GPU (~5h) ACCEPTANCE: Full results JSON, direct comparison table vs T7.
title: T9: Mamba-2 1.3B native SSM baseline

description:
Create the project foundation. Set up pyproject.toml with deps (torch, transformers, reservoirpy, peft, wandb, scipy, ruff, mypy, pytest). Create src/ package layout per IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md Section 7: src/reservoir/, src/models/, src/training/, src/eval/, src/eval/benchmarks/, src/data/. Create tests/ dir. Set up pre-commit with ruff+mypy. Add CI skeleton (.github/workflows/).

Define shared protocols and types in src/types.py (or src/types/):
- ReservoirConfig dataclass (size, spectral_radius, leak_rate, input_scaling, topology)
- Reservoir protocol (step/reset methods)
- ModelWrapper protocol (forward/generate/get_hidden)
- BenchmarkExample dataclass (input/target/metadata)
- EvalResult dataclass (task/metric/value/config)
- Generator protocol for benchmarks
- DataPipeline protocol (iterate/split)

Acceptance: pip install -e . works, pytest runs (even if no tests yet), linter passes, all protocols importable from src.types.

CPU only. No dependencies.
title: T1: Project scaffolding & shared interfaces

description:
Implement src/reservoir/esn.py — ESN class with configurable size (500–50K nodes), spectral radius, leak rate, input scaling, sparse topology (Erdős–Rényi, small-world). Core method: step(x_t, w_t) → r_t for state evolution. Use sparse matrix storage (scipy.sparse or torch.sparse). Support batched forward for GPU tensors.

Key implementation details:
- Initialize weight matrix W with desired spectral radius (generate random sparse, rescale eigenvalues)
- Input weight matrix W_in: dense or sparse, configurable scaling
- State update: r_t = (1-α)*r_{t-1} + α*tanh(W*r_{t-1} + W_in*x_t + w_t)
- α = leak rate
- Implement both Erdős–Rényi (random edges with probability p) and small-world (Watts-Strogatz) topologies
- reset() method to zero state

Tests in tests/test_esn.py:
- Echo state property: spectral radius < 1 ⟹ state fading memory
- Performance: <1 ms per step at 50K nodes on CPU
- State norm bounded over 10K steps across parameter regimes

Depends on: T1 (for types/protocols). CPU only.
title: T2: ESN reservoir core library

description:
Implement src/models/loader.py — unified interface to load models in FP16/BF16:
- Qwen3.5-0.8B-Base (hybrid DeltaNet + full-attention architecture)
- LLaMA-3.2-1B (pure softmax attention)
- Mamba-2 1.3B (SSM architecture)

Features:
- load_model(model_name, dtype, device) → ModelWrapper
- Tokenizer wrappers with consistent API across model families
- Layer-name inspection utilities: list all layer names, identify DeltaNet vs full-attention layers in Qwen3.5, identify LoRA target modules
- get_hidden_states(model, input_ids, layer_indices) utility
- VRAM estimation before loading

The ModelWrapper should implement the protocol from src/types.py (forward/generate/get_hidden).

Acceptance: All three model families load on GPU, forward pass produces logits of correct shape, Qwen3.5 DeltaNet layers identified by name. Add tests in tests/test_loader.py (can be smoke tests that mock model loading for CI).

Depends on: T1 (for ModelWrapper protocol). CPU only (downloads models, no training). Brief GPU smoke test.
title: T3: Model loading & tokenizer utilities

description:
Implement src/eval/harness.py — run any benchmark suite against any model, output standardized JSON.

Features:
- evaluate(model: ModelWrapper, benchmarks: list[Generator], config: EvalConfig) → list[EvalResult]
- Support few-shot prompting (0-shot, k-shot with examples)
- Greedy and sampling decode modes
- Per-task metrics: exact match, perplexity, accuracy, F1
- Batch evaluation for throughput (configurable batch size)
- JSON output format with full reproducibility info (model name, config, timestamp, git hash)
- Progress tracking (tqdm or similar)
- Resume from partial results (append mode)

The harness should be model-agnostic — works with any ModelWrapper. Benchmarks provide (input, target, metadata) via the Generator protocol from src/types.py.

Tests in tests/test_harness.py:
- Harness runs a dummy benchmark against a dummy model
- Produces valid JSON results matching EvalResult schema
- Metrics computed correctly on known inputs

Depends on: T1 (for protocols). CPU only.
title: T4: Evaluation harness

description:
Implement benchmark generators in src/eval/benchmarks/:

src/eval/benchmarks/memory.py:
- Passkey retrieval: hide a passkey at configurable depth in distractor text, query at end. Configurable: context length, passkey position, distractor type.
- Variable tracking: define variables, perform assignments across distractors, query final value. Configurable: num variables, num operations, distractor length.
- Associative recall: present key-value pairs, add distractor, query by key. Configurable: num pairs, delay length.

src/eval/benchmarks/computation.py:
- Multi-digit addition/multiplication with carry (configurable digit count)
- Modular arithmetic (a op b mod m, configurable operand size and modulus)
- Dyck language recognition (balanced parentheses, configurable depth and length)
- Synthetic program execution traces (simple variable assignments and arithmetic)

src/eval/benchmarks/emergent.py:
- Compositional generalization: train on subset of operators, test on held-out combinations
- Length extrapolation: generate at train length, test at 2x-10x length
- Algorithmic transfer: train on one algorithm, test on related algorithm

All generators produce BenchmarkExample(input, target, metadata) tuples with configurable difficulty. Each generator must produce 1K+ valid examples. Format must be compatible with T4 harness (Generator protocol from src/types.py).

Tests in tests/test_benchmarks.py. Depends on: T1. CPU only.
title: T5: Synthetic benchmark suite
